title: Syrmatium veatchii
intro: Syrmatium veatchii, also known as Oregon sunshine or veatch's syrmatium, is a flowering plant in the borage family (Boraginaceae). This plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat various health issues, including inflammation and respiratory problems, due to its anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties. The bioactive constituents of Syrmatium veatchii include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Syrmatium veatchii is often used to make teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are commonly used to alleviate symptoms of colds, fever, and bronchitis.
